howItWorks.intro: The relationship between horsepower, torque, and RPM is defined by a single fundamental formula used throughout automotive and mechanical engineering.
howItWorks.outro: The constant 5,252 comes from unit conversions: 1 HP = 33,000 ft-lbs/min, and since torque is measured in lb-ft while power is in ft-lbs/min, dividing 33,000 by 2π gives 5,252. This is also why horsepower and torque curves always cross at exactly 5,252 RPM on a dyno chart.

faq.question: Why do horsepower and torque always cross at 5,252 RPM?
faq.answer: At 5,252 RPM, the formula HP = Torque × RPM / 5,252 simplifies to HP = Torque. This mathematical relationship means the two values are always equal at exactly 5,252 RPM, regardless of the engine. It's a consequence of the unit definitions, not engine design.

faq.question: What is more important, horsepower or torque?
faq.answer: Torque determines how hard the engine can push (acceleration feel and towing ability), while horsepower determines the top speed and overall rate of work. For towing and off-the-line acceleration, torque matters more. For top speed and high-RPM performance, horsepower matters more.

faq.question: How do I measure my car's horsepower?
faq.answer: A chassis dynamometer (dyno) measures wheel horsepower by spinning the drive wheels against a drum. This gives you actual power at the wheels after drivetrain losses. Crank horsepower (advertised by manufacturers) is typically 15-20% higher than wheel horsepower.

faq.question: What is the difference between crank HP and wheel HP?
faq.answer: Crank HP is measured at the engine's crankshaft and is the number manufacturers advertise. Wheel HP is measured at the wheels after drivetrain losses (transmission, differential, axles). Typical drivetrain loss is 15% for manual and 18-20% for automatic transmissions.

faq.question: Can I convert metric kW to horsepower?
faq.answer: Yes. 1 horsepower (mechanical) = 0.7457 kilowatts. To convert kW to HP, divide by 0.7457 (or multiply by 1.341). For example, 100 kW equals about 134 HP. European cars often list power in kW or PS (Pferdestärke), where 1 PS = 0.9863 HP.

faq.question: Why does a diesel engine have high torque but lower horsepower?
faq.answer: Diesel engines produce high torque at low RPM due to their high compression ratios and long stroke designs. But since HP = Torque × RPM / 5252, the low RPM range means horsepower numbers stay moderate. A diesel making 400 lb-ft at 2,000 RPM only produces about 152 HP.

sections.title: What Is the HP to Torque Calculator?
sections.body: The HP to Torque Calculator is a free online tool designed for car owners and enthusiasts who need quick, accurate calculations in the automotive care and maintenance space. By entering your solve for, horsepower, torque, you get instant results including horsepower, torque, rpm. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting horsepower right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In automotive care and maintenance, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by car owners and enthusiasts worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to maintain your vehicle with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your solve for and need to find the right horsepower. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
